[[af:Aardslang (astrologie)]][[cdo:Gī-sê̤ṳ]][[de:Erde-Schlange]][[fr:Serpent de terre]][[hak:Kí-sṳ]][[ja:己巳]][[ko:기사 (간지)]][[nds:Eer-Snaak]][[vi:Kỷ Tỵ]][[za:Geijceih]][[zh-min-nan:Kí-sū]][[zh-yue:己巳]][[zh:己巳]]

'''Yin Earth Snake also known as 己巳''' (tsuchinotomi, kidonomi, kishi) is one of the elements of the [[Sexagenary cycle]].

It is the 6th combination of the [[Sexagenary cycle]], preceded by [[Yang Earth Dragon]] and followed by [[Yang Metal Horse]]. In the context of [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|Yin Yang Five Elements]], the [[Heavenly Stems]] symbol [[Tsuchinoto (己)]] represents yin [[Earth (wuxing)|Earth (土)]], while the [[Earthly Branches]] symbol [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake (巳)]] represents yin [[Fire (wuxing)|Fire (火)]], establishing a generating relationship ([[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Mutual Generation]]) (i.e. Fire gives rise to Earth).

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Earth Snake (己巳)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jǐsì
** Cantonese (Jyutping): gei2 si6
** Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Tsuchinoto-mi
***Kunyomi 2: Kido-no-mi
***Onyomi 1: kishi
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gisa (기사)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Kỷ Tỵ

==Years of Yin Earth Snake==
A year in the [[Common Era]] that leaves a remainder of 9 when divided by 60 is a [[Year|year]] of [[Yin Earth Snake]].
{| class="wikitable"
|+'''Yin Earth Snake (己巳)'s years table'''
|-
! [[1st millennium|1st Millennium]] !! [[2nd millennium|2nd Millennium]] !! [[3rd millennium|3rd Millennium]]
|-
|
* [[AD 9|9 AD]]
* [[AD 69|69 AD]]
* [[AD 129|129 AD]]
* [[189|189 AD]]
* [[249|249 AD]]
* [[309|309 AD]]
* [[369|369 AD]]
* [[429|429 AD]]
* [[489|489 AD]]
* [[549|549 AD]]
* [[609|609 AD]]
* [[669|669 AD]]
* [[729|729 AD]]
* [[789|789 AD]]
* [[849|849 AD]]
* [[909|909 AD]]
* [[969|969 AD]]
|
* [[1029|1029 AD]]
* [[1089|1089 AD]]
* [[1149|1149 AD]]
* [[1209|1209 AD]]
* [[1269|1269 AD]]
* [[1329|1329 AD]]
* [[1389|1389 AD]]
* [[1449|1449 AD]]
* [[1509|1509 AD]]
* [[1569|1569 AD]]
* [[1629|1629 AD]]
* [[1689|1689 AD]]
* [[1749|1749 AD]]
* [[1809|1809 AD]]
* [[1869|1869 AD]]
* [[1929|1929 AD]]
* [[1989|1989 AD]]
|
* [[2049 AD]]
* [[2100s|2109 AD]]
* [[22nd century|2169 AD]]
* [[23rd century|2229 AD]]
* [[23rd century|2289 AD]]
* [[2349 AD]]
* [[2409 AD]]
* [[2469 AD]]
* [[2529 AD]]
* [[2589 AD]]
* [[2649 AD]]
* [[2709 AD]]
* [[2769 AD]]
* [[2829 AD]]
* [[2889 AD]]
* [[2949 AD]]
|}

===Events===
* [[Kishii Kikou]]: a travelogue of the western regions compiled by [[Kaibara Ekken|Kaihara Ekken]] in [[Genroku]] 2 (1689 AD), notable for being the first known instance of the term [[Three Views of Japan|Japan’s Three Views]]. (As annotated in 『新日本古典文学大系』 by [[Yōko Itasaka]], Iwanami Shoten, 1991)

==Months of Yin Earth Snake==
In a [[Common Era]] year whose final digit is 4 or 9 (when the [[Heavenly Stems]] are [[Kinoe (甲)]] or [[Tsuchinoto (己)]]), the month of [[April]] is designated as the [[Month|month (calendar)]] of [[Yin Earth Snake]]. However, here the term "month" may refer either to the month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] or to the [[Solar term]] (from [[Lixia]] until the day before [[Mangzhong]]) as applicable.

==Days of Yin Earth Snake==
The [[Senjitsu]] for [[Yin Earth Snake]] is as follows.
==={{nihongo|selection days|選日|senjitsu|qq=}}===
The day of [[Yin Earth Snake]] is the 2nd day of [[Tsuchi (Japanese term)|Small Earth]].
===暦注下段===
* For [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] months of April, June, or October, it is an [[Rekichū Gedan]].
* [[Rekichū Gedan]]
* [[Rekichū Gedan]]

==See Also==

{{wikidata link|Q1062039}}
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle]]
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
